builds.disc_notes:
4PC Shining Aria VS 4PC Phaethon Melody: Shining gives 9 less Anomaly Profiency while DMG% buff is the same value. The main difference is that Phaethon's DMG% buff can apply outside of stun and you must remember to use other allies EX during stun to keep up Phaethon's buff; while Aria is easier to manage as its DMG% buff is fully up in stun window.
If you use 4PC Shining Aria, then use 2PC Phaethon for AM%, otherwise use 2PC Freedom/Chaos for the AP.
builds.mindscapes:
M1 allows Basic, Special and EX Special to ignore 10% Buildup RES, not [DMG RES]. Aria's Abloom DMG will now Crit with 25% CRIT DMG value. Note that CRIT Rate won't be capped w/o the AM% from her signature.
M2 allow all attacks and Abloom to ignore 16% DEF, during Ultimate state, ignore another 8% DEF. It stacks well with other DEF shred sources like Sunna's M1.
M6 lets Aria permanently enter Ultimate state, enhanced Basic Attack and Ultimate gain 40 DMG% and stacks are constantly generated.
Best stoping point for lower investment is M0R1 or M1R1
builds.other_notes:
Using Aria's Special Attack (not EX) after specific movesets let you gain 1 Fandom Power. Double Chain Attack on Aria is helpful as she gains Fandom Power stacks from Chain Attacks.
Vesper's -40% CD will not effect Aria's M1 like how it does not affect Jane as Anomaly Crit DMG is a separated multiplier.
builds.team_general: On-field Anomaly: Best in teams with 2 supportive units to boost Abloom DMG. Disorder/ Dual DPS teams are possible but not recommended.

builds.w_engine_notes:
- Cordis Germina is Harumasa's BIS in Dialyn team, giving 25% Elec DMG and DEF ignore on Ultimate. the downside is that it's hard not to overcap CR%, as Harumasa on Seed's sig has 83.4% CR on his Dash Attack already without a single CR% substat roll. His own signature is still better outside of Dialyn team by a bit. 
- Brimstone offers high combat ATK%, and can sometimes outperform Crit statstick as Harumasa can stacks Brimstone buff really well, and he gains a lot of Crit stats from Core Passive.
builds.disc_notes:
- 4PC Shadow Harmony is the most consistent set since he can easily stack the buff from using his Enhanced Dash Attacks. 4PC Puffer Electro is BIS w/ Dialyn team, and 4pc Hormone/Thunder are still good provided you can manage its uptime and condition. 4pc Thunder especially is only used when there's another Elec agents to help with Shock uptime.
- Disc 4 can be either ATK%/CDMG% depend on substats, or CR% If you're lacking too much CR. Disc 5 PEN% is better unless Harumasa is paired with Nicole, in which case, ATK% is usually a bit better. 
¹Crit Rate note: Harumasa gets 25% CR bonus on enhanced Dash Attack, after his Potential Buff, the buff now apply to his Ult and Chasing Thunder, making the max CR 75% and have no more inconsistency between movesets, further reduced by 12% from 4PC Shadow Harmony, and more from teammate or/and W-engine, depending on the setup.
builds.mindscapes:
M1 allows more stacks from your basic attack and your quivers shooting another arrow at the target, which makes him able to do a few more Dashes during stun window, and improving his on field/out of stun capability. M2 gives his own dash attacks 50% DMG buff after using Chain Attack/Ultimate. 
it's recommended to Vertically invest in supportive agent's mindscapes first (such as Dialyn, Trigger, Astra, etc.) before pulling Harumasa's mindscapes.
builds.other_notes:
Harumasa's main game plan is to fit an Ultimate and as many Enhanced Dash Attacks (EDA for short) as possible during the stun window. 
Before entering the battle, Harumasa can pre-generate 6 Electro Quivers via doing his Basic Attack strings 3 times. If there's already 6 Quivers on field, Harumasa can use a Chain Attack/EX Special to generate 6 more Quivers, which makes the previous Quivers fired to the enemy and mark it. by doing this you can save times from his aiming attack, allowing more EDAs in stun.

builds.mindscapes:
M1 gives 10% RES PEN when using One's Path or Earth Shaker, 10% Sheer DMG% when using certain skills; using Crushing Peaks extends stun duration by 2s on stunned target, which can be good for short stun duration target but can lose out another stun when used with Dialyn, making it situational.

builds.other_notes:
High-level gameplay for Banyue requires executing his Perfect Cancel well (adding Basic/EX input at the correct time while another animation is running) to fit in the most DMG. Each version of EX skill has different animation length and scaling, so what combo to use during a stun duration is dependant on the enemy. Focus on fitting the free EX skills and an enhanced EX skill in stun time.
Currently, it's recommended to loop EX: Earth Shaker when out of stun as it has the highest scaling but of course it depend on the flow of battle. Sub-Optimal gameplay can still clear end-game.

builds.mindscapes:
M1 provides significant QoL enabling Caesar to apply her Shield when entering the field without activating it (on a 300s CD), while further applying an All-Attribute RES reduction to nearby enemies.
M2 further increases the ATK buff her Shield provides, by up to 500 ATK more, and slightly increases Caesar's Energy Generation Rate by 10%, increasing her Energy gain from all sources.
builds.other_notes:
Parries, Block Counters and Retaliatory Attacks after Assists and Dodge Counters are the best and fastest way to reapply Caesar's shield and buffs for the team and debuff on nearby targets.
As a Defense agent you will need to use your EX Special even when you do not have energy for EX Special to activate her block counter, Caesar is best used in short bursts to apply Daze/rebuff. 
Reel Projector is not "worth" to level up for Caesar. Levelling a Stun engine has more value, since you can use it on a Stun unit while not using Caesar, and Reel's passive effectively does nothing more than Stun engines do. Consider it an alternative to the A rank stun W-Engines.

builds.mindscapes:
M1 increases her Positive Review generation and amount gained by using her EX Special Attacks by 16%. Agents in the Overwhelmingly Positive state ignore 15% enemy All-Attribute RES.
M2 grants an additional 20% Stun DMG Multiplier on enemies under Malicious Complaint, and the team deals +15% DMG to enemies under that effect.
Going beyond M2 is not recommended.
builds.other_notes:
Dialyn's Chain-to-Ult conversion works as follows: She gains 1 Customer Complaint when an Ultimate is used or upon reaching 90 Positive Reviews. She generates 0.6 Positive Reviews per second and 7.5 per EX.
Upon reaching 90: Triggering a Chain Attack window will convert the next agent’s Chain into their Ultimate, consuming 60 Positive Reviews if done when the enemy is stunned. Alternatively, outside of stun, she can hold BA to consume Customer Complaint, triggering a Quick Assist, and if Dialyn has 90+ Positive Reviews, she triggers a Chain Attack window instead, upgrading the next Chain Attack into an Ultimate.

builds.other_notes: Ye Shunguang plays around her Enlightened state, which can be achieved by getting 6 stack of Qingming Sword Force. Excess Qingming Sword Force stacks are converted to Bearer, which also applies to stacks obtained during the Enlightened state. Upon ending the state, those are converted back to Qingming Sword force up to 3, so make sure to not waste your excess stacks at this point.
